   127→# =============================================================================
   128→# AST Nodes
   129→# =============================================================================
   130→
   131→class ASTNode:
   132→    pass
   133→
   134→
   135→class SignalRef(ASTNode):
   136→    """Reference to a signal with optional slice/index.
   137→
   138→    name: signal name
   139→    msb: None for scalar reference, otherwise MSB of slice/index
   140→    lsb: None for scalar reference, LSB of slice (for single bit, msb=lsb)
   141→    """
   142→    def __init__(self, name: str, msb: Optional[int] = None, lsb: Optional[int] = None):
   143→        self.name = name
   144→        self.msb = msb
   145→        self.lsb = lsb
   146→
   147→    def __repr__(self):
   148→        if self.msb is None:
   149→            return f"SignalRef({self.name})"
   150→        elif self.msb == self.lsb:
   151→            return f"SignalRef({self.name}[{self.msb}])"
   152→        else:
   153→            return f"SignalRef({self.name}[{self.msb}:{self.lsb}])"
   154→
   155→
   156→class Literal(ASTNode):
   157→    """Literal value with width.
   158→
   159→    value: integer value
   160→    width: bit width
   161→    """
   162→    def __init__(self, value: int, width: int):
   163→        self.value = value
   164→        self.width = width
   165→
   166→    def __repr__(self):
   167→        return f"Literal(0b{self.value:0{self.width}b}, width={self.width})"
   168→
   169→
   170→class Concat(ASTNode):
   171→    """Concatenation: {e1, e2, ..., ek}"""
   172→    def __init__(self, parts: List[ASTNode]):
   173→        self.parts = parts
   174→
   175→    def __repr__(self):
   176→        return f"Concat([{', '.join(repr(p) for p in self.parts)}])"
   177→
   178→
   179→class OpCall(ASTNode):
   180→    """Operator call: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)"""
   181→    def __init__(self, op: str, args: List[ASTNode]):
   182→        self.op = op
   183→        self.args = args
   184→
   185→    def __repr__(self):
   186→        return f"OpCall({self.op}, [{', '.join(repr(a) for a in self.args)}])"
   187→
   188→
   189→# =============================================================================
   190→# Signal Declaration
   191→# =============================================================================
   192→
   193→class SignalDecl:
   194→    """Signal declaration with width information.
   195→
   196→    name: signal name
   197→    sig_type: 'input', 'output', or 'wire'
   198→    msb: MSB of the signal (None for scalar in old format)
   199→    lsb: LSB of the signal (None for scalar in old format)
   200→    """
   201→    def __init__(self, name: str, sig_type: str, msb: Optional[int] = None, lsb: Optional[int] = None):
   202→        self.name = name
   203→        self.sig_type = sig_type
   204→        self.msb = msb
   205→        self.lsb = lsb
   206→
   207→    @property
   208→    def width(self) -> int:
   209→        if self.msb is None:
   210→            return 1
   211→        return self.msb - self.lsb + 1
   212→
   213→    def __repr__(self):
   214→        if self.msb is None:
   215→            return f"SignalDecl({self.name}, {self.sig_type})"
   216→        return f"SignalDecl({self.name}[{self.msb}:{self.lsb}], {self.sig_type})"
   217→
   218→
   219→# =============================================================================
   220→# Token Types
   221→# =============================================================================
   222→
   223→TOKEN_ID = 'ID'
   224→TOKEN_KW = 'KW'
   225→TOKEN_OP = 'OP'
   226→TOKEN_LITERAL = 'LITERAL'
   227→TOKEN_NUMBER = 'NUMBER'
   228→TOKEN_LBRACE = '{'
   229→TOKEN_RBRACE = '}'
   230→TOKEN_LBRACK = '['
   231→TOKEN_RBRACK = ']'
   232→TOKEN_COLON = ':'
   233→TOKEN_EQ = '='
   234→TOKEN_COMMA = ','
   235→TOKEN_LPAREN = '('
   236→TOKEN_RPAREN = ')'
   237→TOKEN_EOF = 'EOF'
   238→
   239→
   240→class Token:
   241→    def __init__(self, type: str, value: str, line: int, col: int):
   242→        self.type = type
   243→        self.value = value
   244→        self.line = line
   245→        self.col = col
   246→
   247→    def __repr__(self):
   248→        return f"Token({self.type}, {self.value!r}, line={self.line}, col={self.col})"
   249→
   250→
   251→# =============================================================================
   252→# Lexer
   253→# =============================================================================
   254→
   255→def tokenize(line: str, line_num: int) -> List[Token]:
   256→    """Tokenize a single line.
   257→
   258→    Returns list of Token objects.
   259→    """
   260→    tokens = []
   261→    pos = 0
   262→    length = len(line)
   263→
   264→    # Operator patterns (case-insensitive)
   265→    operators = {'AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR', 'NOT', 'BUF',
   266→                 'MUX', 'ITE', 'REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR', 'EQ'}
   267→
   268→    while pos < length:
   269→        col_pos = pos + 1  # 1-based column
   270→
   271→        # Skip whitespace
   272→        if line[pos] in ' \t':
   273→            pos += 1
   274→            continue
   275→
   276→        # Comment
   277→        if line[pos] == '#':
   278→            break  # Rest of line is comment, ignore
   279→
   280→        # Identifiers (letters, underscores, digits after first char)
   281→        if re.match(r'[a-zA-Z_]', line[pos:]):
   282→            match = re.match(r'[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*', line[pos:])
   283→            if match:
   284→                token = match.group()
   285→                upper_token = token.upper()
   286→                if upper_token in operators:
   287→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_OP, upper_token, line_num, col_pos))
   288→                elif upper_token in {'INPUT', 'OUTPUT', 'WIRE'}:
   289→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_KW, upper_token, line_num, col_pos))
   290→                else:
   291→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_ID, token, line_num, col_pos))
   292→                pos += len(token)
   293→                continue
   294→
   295→        # Numbers (decimal integers for msb:lsb notation)
   296→        if re.match(r'[0-9]', line[pos:]):
   297→            match = re.match(r'[0-9]+', line[pos:])
   298→            if match:
   299→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_NUMBER, match.group(), line_num, col_pos))
   300→                pos += len(match.group())
   301→                continue
   302→
   303→        # Literals: 0, 1 (scalar), sized/unsized vectors
   304→        if line[pos] in '01':
   305→            # Check if this is a sized literal like 8'b1010
   306→            rest = line[pos:]
   307→            sized_match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', rest)
   308→            if sized_match:
   309→                # This is a sized literal
   310→                width = int(sized_match.group(1))
   311→                radix_str = sized_match.group(2).lower()
   312→                value_str = sized_match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   313→
   314→                # Parse based on radix
   315→                if radix_str in ('b', 'h', 'd'):
   316→                    try:
   317→                        if radix_str == 'b':
   318→                            value = int(value_str, 2)
   319→                        elif radix_str == 'h':
   320→                            value = int(value_str, 16)
   321→                        else:
   322→                            value = int(value_str, 10)
   323→                    except ValueError:
   324→                        raise CircParseError(
   325→                            f"Invalid literal: {sized_match.group(0)}",
   326→                            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   327→                        )
   328→
   329→                    # Check value fits in width
   330→                    if value >= (1 << width):
   331→                        raise CircParseError(
   332→                            f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal",
   333→                            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   334→                        )
   335→
   336→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, f"{width}'{radix_str}{value_str}", line_num, col_pos))
   337→                    pos += len(sized_match.group(0))
   338→                    continue
   339→            else:
   340→                # Scalar literal
   341→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   342→                pos += 1
   343→                continue
   344→
   345→        # Check for 0b or 0x binary/hex literals
   346→        if line[pos:pos+2] in ('0b', '0x'):
   347→            prefix = line[pos:pos+2]
   348→            rest = line[pos+2:]
   349→            # Must have at least one digit after prefix
   350→            match = re.match(r'[0-9a-fA-F]+', rest)
   351→            if match:
   352→                digits = match.group()
   353→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, f"{prefix}{digits}", line_num, col_pos))
   354→                pos += 2 + len(digits)
   355→                continue
   356→            else:
   357→                raise CircParseError(
   358→                    f"Binary/hex literal must have at least one digit",
   359→                    file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   360→                )
   361→
   362→        # Braces and brackets
   363→        if line[pos] in '{}[]':
   364→            tokens.append(Token(line[pos], line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   365→            pos += 1
   366→            continue
   367→
   368→        # Colon
   369→        if line[pos] == ':':
   370→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_COLON, ':', line_num, col_pos))
   371→            pos += 1
   372→            continue
   373→
   374→        # Comma
   375→        if line[pos] == ',':
   376→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_COMMA, ',', line_num, col_pos))
   377→            pos += 1
   378→            continue
   379→
   380→        # Equals sign
   381→        if line[pos] == '=':
   382→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_EQ, '=', line_num, col_pos))
   383→            pos += 1
   384→            continue
   385→
   386→        # Parentheses
   387→        if line[pos] in '()':
   388→            type_map = {'(': TOKEN_LPAREN, ')': TOKEN_RPAREN}
   389→            tokens.append(Token(type_map[line[pos]], line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   390→            pos += 1
   391→            continue
   392→
   393→        # Unknown character
   394→        raise CircParseError(
   395→            f"Unexpected character: {line[pos]}",
   396→            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   397→        )
   398→
   399→    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_EOF, '', line_num, len(line) + 1))
   400→    return tokens
   401→
   402→
   403→# =============================================================================
   404→# Parser
   405→# =============================================================================
   406→
   407→class Parser:
   408→    """Parser for circuit expressions."""
   409→
   410→    def __init__(self, tokens: List[Token], filepath: str):
   411→        self.tokens = tokens
   412→        self.filepath = filepath
   413→        self.pos = 0
   414→
   415→    def peek(self) -> Token:
   416→        return self.tokens[self.pos]
   417→
   418→    def consume(self, expected_type: Optional[str] = None,
   419→                expected_value: Optional[str] = None) -> Token:
   420→        tok = self.peek()
   421→        if expected_type is not None and tok.type != expected_type:
   422→            raise self.error(f"Expected token type {expected_type}, got {tok.type}")
   423→        if expected_value is not None and tok.value != expected_value:
   424→            raise self.error(f"Expected {expected_value}, got {tok.value}")
   425→        self.pos += 1
   426→        return tok
   427→
   428→    def error(self, message: str) -> CircParseError:
   429→        tok = self.peek()
   430→        return CircParseError(
   431→            message,
   432→            file=self.filepath,
   433→            line=tok.line,
   434→            col=tok.col
   435→        )
   436→
   437→    def parse_expression(self) -> ASTNode:
   438→        """Parse an expression: concatenation, signal ref, literal, or op call."""
   439→        tok = self.peek()
   440→
   441→        # If it's an identifier followed by [, it's a slice/index
   442→        if tok.type == TOKEN_ID:
   443→            name = tok.value
   444→            self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   445→
   446→            # Check for slice or index
   447→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_LBRACK:
   448→                return self.parse_signal_ref_with_select(name)
   449→            else:
   450→                return SignalRef(name)
   451→
   452→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_LITERAL:
   453→            return self.parse_literal()
   454→
   455→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_LBRACE:
   456→            # Concatenation: {e1, e2, ...}
   457→            return self.parse_concat()
   458→
   459→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_OP:
   460→            # Operator call: OP(...)
   461→            return self.parse_op_call()
   462→
   463→        else:
   464→            raise self.error(f"Unexpected token in expression: {tok.value}")
   465→
   466→    def parse_signal_ref_with_select(self, name: str) -> SignalRef:
   467→        """Parse a signal reference with optional slice/index: v[i] or v[hi:lo]"""
   468→        self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACK)
   469→
   470→        # Parse first number (msb or index)
   471→        if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   472→            raise self.error("Expected number in slice/index")
   473→        msb_val = int(self.consume().value)
   474→
   475→        # Check if it's a slice (with colon) or single bit index
   476→        if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COLON:
   477→            self.consume(TOKEN_COLON)  # consume ':'
   478→
   479→            # Parse lsb
   480→            if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   481→                raise self.error("Expected number after colon in slice")
   482→            lsb_val = int(self.consume().value)
   483→
   484→            self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   485→
   486→            # Validate: msb >= lsb >= 0
   487→            if msb_val < lsb_val:
   488→                raise self.error(f"Invalid slice: msb ({msb_val}) < lsb ({lsb_val})")
   489→            if lsb_val < 0:
   490→                raise self.error(f"Invalid slice: lsb ({lsb_val}) < 0")
   491→
   492→            return SignalRef(name, msb_val, lsb_val)
   493→        else:
   494→            # Single bit index
   495→            self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   496→            return SignalRef(name, msb_val, msb_val)  # msb=lsb for single bit
   497→
   498→    def parse_literal(self) -> Literal:
   499→        """Parse a literal value."""
   500→        tok = self.consume(TOKEN_LITERAL)
   501→        val = tok.value
   502→
   503→        if val in ('0', '1'):
   504→            return Literal(int(val), 1)
   505→        elif val.startswith('0b'):
   506→            # Binary literal: 0b1101
   507→            bits = val[2:]
   508→            if not bits:
   509→                raise self.error("Binary literal must have at least one digit")
   510→            value = int(bits, 2)
   511→            return Literal(value, len(bits))
   512→        elif val.startswith('0x'):
   513→            # Hex literal: 0x0f
   514→            hex_digits = val[2:]
   515→            if not hex_digits:
   516→                raise self.error("Hex literal must have at least one digit")
   517→            value = int(hex_digits, 16)
   518→            return Literal(value, len(hex_digits) * 4)
   519→        else:
   520→            # Sized literal: 8'b1010, 8'hff, 8'd200
   521→            match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', val)
   522→            if match:
   523→                width = int(match.group(1))
   524→                radix_str = match.group(2).lower()
   525→                value_str = match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   526→
   527→                try:
   528→                    if radix_str == 'b':
   529→                        value = int(value_str, 2)
   530→                    elif radix_str == 'h':
   531→                        value = int(value_str, 16)
   532→                    else:  # 'd'
   533→                        value = int(value_str, 10)
   534→                except ValueError:
   535→                    raise self.error(f"Invalid literal: {val}")
   536→
   537→                if value >= (1 << width):
   538→                    raise self.error(f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal")
   539→
   540→                return Literal(value, width)
   541→            else:
   542→                raise self.error(f"Invalid literal format: {val}")
   543→
   544→    def parse_concat(self) -> Concat:
   545→        """Parse concatenation: {e1, e2, ...}"""
   546→        self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACE)
   547→
   548→        parts = []
   549→        while True:
   550→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_RBRACE:
   551→                break
   552→
   553→            parts.append(self.parse_expression())
   554→
   555→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   556→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   557→                continue
   558→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_RBRACE:
   559→                break
   560→
   561→        self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACE)
   562→        return Concat(parts)
   563→
   564→    def parse_op_call(self) -> OpCall:
   565→        """Parse operator call: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)"""
   566→        op_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_OP)
   567→        op = op_tok.value
   568→
   569→        self.consume(TOKEN_LPAREN)
   570→
   571→        args = []
   572→        while True:
   573→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_RPAREN:
   574→                break
   575→
   576→            args.append(self.parse_expression())
   577→
   578→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   579→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   580→                continue
   581→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_RPAREN:
   582→                break
   583→
   584→        self.consume(TOKEN_RPAREN)
   585→        return OpCall(op, args)
   586→
   587→    def parse_assignment_line(self) -> Tuple[str, ASTNode]:
   588→        """Parse an assignment line: lhs = expression"""
   589→        # Parse LHS (identifier)
   590→        lhs_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   591→        lhs = lhs_tok.value
   592→
   593→        self.consume(TOKEN_EQ)
   594→
   595→        # Parse RHS expression
   596→        expr = self.parse_expression()
   597→
   598→        # Make sure we're at EOF
   599→        if self.peek().type != TOKEN_EOF:
   600→            raise self.error(f"Unexpected token after expression: {self.peek().value}")
   601→
   602→        return lhs, expr
   603→
   604→    def parse_declaration(self) -> List[SignalDecl]:
   605→        """Parse a declaration line: INPUT/OUTPUT/WIRE name[msb:lsb]"""
   606→        kw_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_KW)
   607→        decl_type = kw_tok.value.lower()
   608→
   609→        decls = []
   610→        while True:
   611→            # Parse identifier
   612→            id_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   613→            name = id_tok.value
   614→
   615→            # Check for width specifier
   616→            msb = None
   617→            lsb = None
   618→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_LBRACK:
   619→                self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACK)
   620→
   621→                # Parse msb
   622→                if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   623→                    raise self.error(f"Expected number after [ for {name}")
   624→                msb = int(self.consume().value)
   625→
   626→                # Check for slice or single bit
   627→                if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COLON:
   628→                    self.consume(TOKEN_COLON)
   629→
   630→                    if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   631→                        raise self.error(f"Expected number after : for {name}")
   632→                    lsb = int(self.consume().value)
   633→
   634→                    if msb < lsb:
   635→                        raise self.error(f"Invalid range for {name}: msb ({msb}) < lsb ({lsb})")
   636→                    if lsb < 0:
   637→                        raise self.error(f"Invalid range for {name}: lsb ({lsb}) < 0")
   638→                else:
   639→                    # Single bit - msb=lsb
   640→                    lsb = msb
   641→
   642→                self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   643→
   644→            decls.append(SignalDecl(name, decl_type, msb, lsb))
   645→
   646→            # Check for more names or end of line
   647→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_EOF:
   648→                break
   649→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   650→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   651→                continue
   652→            else:
   653→                raise self.error(f"Unexpected token: {self.peek().value}")
   654→
   655→        return decls

   658→def parse_circ_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
   659→    """Parse a .circ file and return (declarations, assignments).
   660→
   661→    Returns:
   662→        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects
   663→        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples
   664→    """
   665→    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
   666→        lines = f.readlines()
   667→
   668→    declarations = []
   669→    assignments = []
   670→    seen_assignment = False
   671→
   672→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   673→        line = line.rstrip('\n\r')
   674→
   675→        # Skip empty lines and comments
   676→        stripped = line.lstrip()
   677→        if not stripped or stripped.startswith('#'):
   678→            continue
   679→
   680→        try:
   681→            tokens = tokenize(line, line_num)
   682→        except CircParseError as e:
   683→            if e.file is None:
   684→                e.file = filepath
   685→            raise
   686→
   687→        if not tokens:
   688→            continue
   689→
   690→        parser = Parser(tokens, filepath)
   691→
   692→        # Check if it's a declaration
   693→        if tokens[0].type == TOKEN_KW:
   694→            # Declaration line
   695→            if seen_assignment:
   696→                raise DeclarationAfterAssignmentError(
   697→                    f"Declaration found after assignment",
   698→                    file=filepath,
   699→                    line=line_num,
   700→                    col=1
   701→                )
   702→
   703→            try:
   704→                decls = parser.parse_declaration()
   705→                declarations.extend(decls)
   706→            except CircParseError as e:
   707→                if e.file is None:
   708→                    e.file = filepath
   709→                raise
   710→
   711→        elif tokens[0].type == TOKEN_ID:
   712→            # Assignment line
   713→            seen_assignment = True
   714→
   715→            try:
   716→                lhs, expr = parser.parse_assignment_line()
   717→                assignments.append((lhs, expr))
   718→            except CircParseError as e:
   719→                if e.file is None:
   720→                    e.file = filepath
   721→                raise
   722→        else:
   723→            raise CircParseError(
   724→                f"Expected keyword (input/output/wire) or identifier",
   725→                file=filepath,
   726→                line=line_num,
   727→                col=1
   728→            )
   729→
   730→    return declarations, assignments

   734→# JSON Circuit Format Parser
   735→# =============================================================================
   736→
   737→def parse_json_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
   738→    """Parse a JSON circuit file and return (declarations, assignments).
   739→
   740→    Returns:
   741→        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects
   742→        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples
   743→
   744→    Raises:
   745→        JsonParseError: Invalid JSON syntax
   746→        JsonSchemaError: JSON doesn't match expected schema
   747→        CircParseError: Expression parsing error
   748→    """
   749→    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
   750→        try:
   751→            data = json.load(f)
   752→        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
   753→            raise JsonParseError(
   754→                f"Invalid JSON syntax: {e}",
   755→                file=filepath,
   756→                line=e.lineno,
   757→                col=e.colno
   758→            )
   759→
   760→    # Validate schema
   761→    if not isinstance(data, dict):
   762→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   763→            "JSON root must be an object",
   764→            file=filepath
   765→        )
   766→
   767→    required_keys = {'format_version', 'inputs', 'outputs', 'wires', 'assignments'}
   768→    missing = required_keys - set(data.keys())
   769→    if missing:
   770→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   771→            f"Missing required keys: {', '.join(sorted(missing))}",
   772→            file=filepath
   773→        )
   774→
   775→    # Check format_version
   776→    if data['format_version'] != 1:
   777→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   778→            f"Unsupported format_version: {data['format_version']}. Expected 1.",
   779→            file=filepath
   780→        )
   781→
   782→    # Helper to validate port object
   783→    def validate_port(port: dict, key_name: str) -> dict:
   784→        if not isinstance(port, dict):
   785→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   786→                f"{key_name} must be an array of objects",
   787→                file=filepath
   788→            )
   789→        if 'name' not in port:
   790→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   791→                f"Port object missing 'name' field",
   792→                file=filepath
   793→            )
   794→        msb = port.get('msb')
   795→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   796→
   797→        if msb is None and lsb is None:
   798→            # Scalar port - valid
   799→            return port
   800→
   801→        if not isinstance(msb, int) or not isinstance(lsb, int):
   802→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   803→                f"Port '{port.get('name', '?')}': msb and lsb must be integers",
   804→                file=filepath
   805→            )
   806→
   807→        if msb < lsb:
   808→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   809→                f"Port '{port['name']}': msb ({msb}) < lsb ({lsb})",
   810→                file=filepath
   811→            )
   812→
   813→        if lsb < 0:
   814→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   815→                f"Port '{port['name']}': lsb ({lsb}) < 0",
   816→                file=filepath
   817→            )
   818→
   819→        return port
   820→
   821→    # Parse inputs
   822→    inputs_list = data['inputs']
   823→    if not isinstance(inputs_list, list):
   824→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   825→            "'inputs' must be an array",
   826→            file=filepath
   827→        )
   828→
   829→    declarations: List[SignalDecl] = []
   830→    seen_names: Set[str] = set()
   831→
   832→    for i, port in enumerate(inputs_list):
   833→        validate_port(port, f"inputs[{i}]")
   834→        name = port['name']
   835→        if name in seen_names:
   836→            raise DuplicateNameError(
   837→                f"Duplicate input name: {name}",
   838→                file=filepath
   839→            )
   840→        seen_names.add(name)
   841→
   842→        msb = port.get('msb')
   843→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   844→        # Convert None/None to None, None for scalar; otherwise keep values
   845→        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
   846→        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
   847→        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'input', msb_val, lsb_val))
   848→
   849→    # Parse outputs
   850→    outputs_list = data['outputs']
   851→    if not isinstance(outputs_list, list):
   852→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   853→            "'outputs' must be an array",
   854→            file=filepath
   855→        )
   856→
   857→    for i, port in enumerate(outputs_list):
   858→        validate_port(port, f"outputs[{i}]")
   859→        name = port['name']
   860→        if name in seen_names:
   861→            raise DuplicateNameError(
   862→                f"Duplicate output name: {name}",
   863→                file=filepath
   864→            )
   865→        seen_names.add(name)
   866→
   867→        msb = port.get('msb')
   868→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   869→        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
   870→        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
   871→        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'output', msb_val, lsb_val))
   872→
   873→    # Parse wires
   874→    wires_list = data['wires']
   875→    if not isinstance(wires_list, list):
   876→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   877→            "'wires' must be an array",
   878→            file=filepath
   879→        )
   880→
   881→    for i, port in enumerate(wires_list):
   882→        validate_port(port, f"wires[{i}]")
   883→        name = port['name']
   884→        if name in seen_names:
   885→            raise DuplicateNameError(
   886→                f"Duplicate wire name: {name}",
   887→                file=filepath
   888→            )
   889→        seen_names.add(name)
   890→
   891→        msb = port.get('msb')
   892→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   893→        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
   894→        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
   895→        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'wire', msb_val, lsb_val))
   896→
   897→    # Parse assignments
   898→    assignments_list = data['assignments']
   899→    if not isinstance(assignments_list, list):
   900→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   901→            "'assignments' must be an array",
   902→            file=filepath
   903→        )
   904→
   905→    assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]] = []
   906→    lhs_seen: Set[str] = set()
   907→
   908→    for i, assn in enumerate(assignments_list):
   909→        if not isinstance(assn, dict):
   910→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   911→                f"assignments[{i}] must be an object",
   912→                file=filepath
   913→            )
   914→        if 'lhs' not in assn:
   915→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   916→                f"assignments[{i}] missing 'lhs' field",
   917→                file=filepath
   918→            )
   919→        if 'rhs' not in assn:
   920→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   921→                f"assignments[{i}] missing 'rhs' field",
   922→                file=filepath
   923→            )
   924→
   925→        lhs = assn['lhs']
   926→        rhs_str = assn['rhs']
   927→
   928→        if not isinstance(lhs, str):
   929→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   930→                f"assignments[{i}].lhs must be a string",
   931→                file=filepath
   932→            )
   933→        if not isinstance(rhs_str, str):
   934→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   935→                f"assignments[{i}].rhs must be a string",
   936→                file=filepath
   937→            )
   938→
   939→        if lhs in lhs_seen:
   940→            raise RedefinitionError(
   941→                f"Signal '{lhs}' defined multiple times",
   942→                file=filepath
   943→            )
   944→        lhs_seen.add(lhs)
   945→
   946→        # Parse RHS expression using existing expression parser
   947→        # Create a temporary tokens list by tokenizing the expression string
   948→        # We'll reuse the existing tokenize function by creating a mock line
   949→        try:
   950→            tokens = tokenize(rhs_str, 1)  # line 1 for expression error context
   951→        except CircParseError as e:
   952→            # Adjust line/col to point to the assignment
   953→            raise CircParseError(
   954→                f"Error in assignment {i}: {e.message}",
   955→                file=filepath,
   956→                line=None,
   957→                col=None
   958→            )
   959→
   960→        parser = Parser(tokens, filepath)
   961→        try:
   962→            expr = parser.parse_expression()
   963→        except CircParseError as e:
   964→            raise CircParseError(
   965→                f"Error in assignment {i}: {e.message}",
   966→                file=filepath
   967→            )
   968→
   969→        # Ensure we consumed all tokens (no trailing junk)
   970→        if parser.peek().type != TOKEN_EOF:
   971→            raise CircParseError(
   972→                f"Unexpected token after expression in assignment {i}",
   973→                file=filepath
   974→            )
   975→
   976→        assignments.append((lhs, expr))
   977→
   978→    # Validate that every wire and output appears exactly once as assignment LHS
   979→    wire_names = {d.name for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'wire'}
   980→    output_names = {d.name for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'output'}
   981→    assigned_names = set(lhs_seen)
   982→
   983→    unassigned = (wire_names | output_names) - assigned_names
   984→    if unassigned:
   985→        raise UnassignedSignalError(
   986→            f"Unassigned signals: {', '.join(sorted(unassigned))}",
   987→            file=filepath
   988→        )
   989→
   990→    # Check for extra assignments (LHS not declared)
   991→    declared_names = {d.name for d in declarations}
   992→    extra_assigned = assigned_names - declared_names
   993→    if extra_assigned:
   994→        raise UndefinedNameError(
   995→            f"Assignment to undeclared signal(s): {', '.join(sorted(extra_assigned))}",
   996→            file=filepath
   997→        )
   998→
   999→    return declarations, assignments

  1003→# BENCH Circuit Format Parser
  1004→# =============================================================================
  1005→
  1006→def parse_bench_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
  1007→    """Parse a BENCH format circuit file and return (declarations, assignments).
  1008→
  1009→    BENCH is a scalar-only format commonly used in circuit benchmarks.
  1010→
  1011→    Returns:
  1012→        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects (all scalar)
  1013→        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples
  1014→
  1015→    Raises:
  1016→        BenchParseError: Syntax error in .bench file
  1017→        RedefinitionError: Signal defined multiple times
  1018→        UndefinedNameError: Undefined name referenced
  1019→    """
  1020→    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
  1021→        lines = f.readlines()
  1022→
  1023→    declarations: List[SignalDecl] = []
  1024→    assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]] = []
  1025→
  1026→    # Track all signals (inputs, wires, outputs)
  1027→    signal_names: Set[str] = set()
  1028→    input_names: Set[str] = set()
  1029→    output_names: Set[str] = set()
  1030→    wire_names: Set[str] = set()
  1031→
  1032→    # Track assigned signals (for RedefinitionError)
  1033→    assigned_lhs: Set[str] = set()
  1034→
  1035→    # For expression parsing
  1036→    op_arity = {
  1037→        'NOT': 1,
  1038→        'BUF': 1,
  1039→        'AND': 2,
  1040→        'OR': 2,
  1041→        'XOR': 2,
  1042→        'NAND': 2,
  1043→        'NOR': 2,
  1044→        'XNOR': 2,
  1045→    }
  1046→
  1047→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
  1048→        line = line.rstrip('\n\r')
  1049→
  1050→        # Strip comment
  1051→        if '#' in line:
  1052→            line = line[:line.index('#')]
  1053→
  1054→        # Skip empty lines
  1055→        stripped = line.strip()
  1056→        if not stripped:
  1057→            continue
  1058→
  1059→        # Remove all whitespace for easier parsing
  1060→        no_whitespace = ''.join(stripped.split())
  1061→
  1062→        # Check for INPUT(name)
  1063→        if no_whitespace.startswith('INPUT(') and no_whitespace.endswith(')'):
  1064→            name = no_whitespace[6:-1]  # Remove 'INPUT(' and ')'
  1065→            if not name:
  1066→                raise BenchParseError(
  1067→                    f"Empty input name at line {line_num}",
  1068→                    file=filepath,
  1069→                    line=line_num,
  1070→                    col=1
  1071→                )
  1072→
  1073→            # Check for brackets (vector support not allowed in BENCH)
  1074→            if '[' in name or ']' in name:
  1075→                raise BenchParseError(
  1076→                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {name}",
  1077→                    file=filepath,
  1078→                    line=line_num,
  1079→                    col=1
  1080→                )
  1081→
  1082→            if name in signal_names:
  1083→                raise RedefinitionError(
  1084→                    f"Duplicate signal definition: {name}",
  1085→                    file=filepath,
  1086→                    line=line_num,
  1087→                    col=1
  1088→                )
  1089→
  1090→            signal_names.add(name)
  1091→            input_names.add(name)
  1092→            declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'input', None, None))
  1093→            continue
  1094→
  1095→        # Check for OUTPUT(name)
  1096→        if no_whitespace.startswith('OUTPUT(') and no_whitespace.endswith(')'):
  1097→            name = no_whitespace[7:-1]  # Remove 'OUTPUT(' and ')'
  1098→            if not name:
  1099→                raise BenchParseError(
  1100→                    f"Empty output name at line {line_num}",
  1101→                    file=filepath,
  1102→                    line=line_num,
  1103→                    col=1
  1104→                )
  1105→
  1106→            if '[' in name or ']' in name:
  1107→                raise BenchParseError(
  1108→                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {name}",
  1109→                    file=filepath,
  1110→                    line=line_num,
  1111→                    col=1
  1112→                )
  1113→
  1114→            if name in signal_names:
  1115→                raise RedefinitionError(
  1116→                    f"Duplicate signal definition: {name}",
  1117→                    file=filepath,
  1118→                    line=line_num,
  1119→                    col=1
  1120→                )
  1121→
  1122→            signal_names.add(name)
  1123→            output_names.add(name)
  1124→            # Add as output declaration
  1125→            declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'output', None, None))
  1126→            continue
  1127→
  1128→        # Check for assignment: lhs = OP(arg1, arg2, ...)
  1129→        if '=' in no_whitespace:
  1130→            parts = no_whitespace.split('=', 1)
  1131→            if len(parts) != 2:
  1132→                raise BenchParseError(
  1133→                    f"Invalid assignment at line {line_num}",
  1134→                    file=filepath,
  1135→                    line=line_num,
  1136→                    col=1
  1137→                )
  1138→
  1139→            lhs = parts[0]
  1140→            rhs = parts[1]
  1141→
  1142→            # Validate LHS
  1143→            if not lhs:
  1144→                raise BenchParseError(
  1145→                    f"Empty LHS in assignment at line {line_num}",
  1146→                    file=filepath,
  1147→                    line=line_num,
  1148→                    col=1
  1149→                )
  1150→
  1151→            if '[' in lhs or ']' in lhs:
  1152→                raise BenchParseError(
  1153→                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {lhs}",
  1154→                    file=filepath,
  1155→                    line=line_num,
  1156→                    col=1
  1157→                )
  1158→
  1159→            # Check for redefinition
  1160→            if lhs in assigned_lhs:
  1161→                raise RedefinitionError(
  1162→                    f"Redefinition of signal: {lhs}",
  1163→                    file=filepath,
  1164→                    line=line_num,
  1165→                    col=1
  1166→                )
  1167→
  1168→            assigned_lhs.add(lhs)
  1169→
  1170→            # Add as wire if not already declared as input/output
  1171→            if lhs not in signal_names:
  1172→                signal_names.add(lhs)
  1173→                wire_names.add(lhs)
  1174→                declarations.append(SignalDecl(lhs, 'wire', None, None))
  1175→
  1176→            # Parse RHS: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)
  1177→            # Find operator name
  1178→            op_match = None
  1179→            for op in op_arity:
  1180→                if rhs.startswith(op + '(') and rhs.endswith(')'):
  1181→                    op_match = op
  1182→                    break
  1183→
  1184→            if op_match is None:
  1185→                raise BenchParseError(
  1186→                    f"Unknown or malformed operator call: {rhs}",
  1187→                    file=filepath,
  1188→                    line=line_num,
  1189→                    col=1
  1190→                )
  1191→
  1192→            args_str = rhs[len(op_match)+1:-1]  # Remove OP( and )
  1193→            if args_str:
  1194→                arg_names = [arg.strip() for arg in args_str.split(',')]
  1195→            else:
  1196→                arg_names = []
  1197→
  1198→            # Validate argument count
  1199→            min_arity = op_arity[op_match]
  1200→            if len(arg_names) < min_arity:
  1201→                raise BenchParseError(
  1202→                    f"Operator {op_match} requires at least {min_arity} operands, got {len(arg_names)}",
  1203→                    file=filepath,
  1204→                    line=line_num,
  1205→                    col=1
  1206→                )
  1207→
  1208→            # Check for literals (BENCH doesn't allow literals in RHS)
  1209→            for arg in arg_names:
  1210→                # Literals: 0, 1, 0b..., 0x..., sized literals
  1211→                if arg in ('0', '1'):
  1212→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1213→                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1214→                        file=filepath,
  1215→                        line=line_num,
  1216→                        col=1
  1217→                    )
  1218→                if arg.startswith('0b') or arg.startswith('0x'):
  1219→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1220→                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1221→                        file=filepath,
  1222→                        line=line_num,
  1223→                        col=1
  1224→                    )
  1225→                if re.match(r'^\d+[\'[bBhHdD]', arg):
  1226→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1227→                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1228→                        file=filepath,
  1229→                        line=line_num,
  1230→                        col=1
  1231→                    )
  1232→
  1233→                # Check for brackets (vector not allowed)
  1234→                if '[' in arg or ']' in arg:
  1235→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1236→                        f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1237→                        file=filepath,
  1238→                        line=line_num,
  1239→                        col=1
  1240→                    )
  1241→
  1242→            # Build AST: OpCall with SignalRef arguments
  1243→            args_ast = []
  1244→            for arg in arg_names:
  1245→                # BENCH is scalar-only, no indices/slices
  1246→                args_ast.append(SignalRef(arg))
  1247→
  1248→            expr = OpCall(op_match, args_ast)
  1249→            assignments.append((lhs, expr))
  1250→            continue
  1251→
  1252→        # If we get here, the line is invalid
  1253→        raise BenchParseError(
  1254→            f"Invalid statement: {stripped}",
  1255→            file=filepath,
  1256→            line=line_num,
  1257→            col=1
  1258→        )
  1259→
  1260→    # Validate OUTPUT references
  1261→    for out_name in output_names:
  1262→        if out_name not in signal_names:
  1263→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1264→                f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {out_name}",
  1265→                file=filepath
  1266→            )
  1267→        # Also check that OUTPUT is assigned somewhere (either as input or assigned)
  1268→        if out_name not in assigned_lhs and out_name not in input_names:
  1269→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1270→                f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {out_name}",
  1271→                file=filepath
  1272→            )
  1273→
  1274→    # Validate that all assigned signals (except inputs) are defined
  1275→    for lhs in assigned_lhs:
  1276→        if lhs not in input_names and lhs not in wire_names:
  1277→            # Already added as wire, but check consistency
  1278→            pass
  1279→
  1280→    # Also: wires must be assigned (already tracked via assigned_lhs)
  1281→    for wname in wire_names:
  1282→        if wname not in assigned_lhs:
  1283→            raise UnassignedSignalError(
  1284→                f"Unassigned wire: {wname}",
  1285→                file=filepath
  1286→            )
  1287→
  1288→    return declarations, assignments
  1289→
  1290→
  1291→# =============================================================================
  1292→# Expression Evaluator (for circuit evaluation)
  1293→# =============================================================================
  1294→
  1295→def eval_expression(expr: ASTNode, signal_values: Dict[str, int],
  1296→                    signal_widths: Dict[str, int],
  1297→                    filepath: str) -> Tuple[int, int]:
  1298→    """Evaluate an expression and return (value, width).
  1299→
  1300→    Args:
  1301→        expr: AST node to evaluate
  1302→        signal_values: Dict mapping signal names to their integer values
  1303→        signal_widths: Dict mapping signal names to their widths
  1304→        filepath: For error reporting
  1305→
  1306→    Returns:
  1307→        Tuple of (value, width)
  1308→    """
  1309→    if isinstance(expr, SignalRef):
  1310→        if expr.name in signal_values:
  1311→            # Get the full signal value
  1312→            full_value = signal_values[expr.name]
  1313→            width = signal_widths[expr.name]
  1314→
  1315→            if expr.msb is None:
  1316→                # Scalar reference
  1317→                return full_value, 1
  1318→            else:
  1319→                # Slice or single bit
  1320→                if expr.msb >= width or expr.lsb >= width:
  1321→                    raise IndexOutOfBoundsError(
  1322→                        f"Index {expr.msb}:{expr.lsb} out of bounds for signal {expr.name} (width {width})",
  1323→                        file=filepath
  1324→                    )
  1325→                # Extract bits from lsb to msb
  1326→                mask = ((1 << (expr.msb - expr.lsb + 1)) - 1) << expr.lsb
  1327→                slice_value = (full_value & mask) >> expr.lsb
  1328→                return slice_value, expr.msb - expr.lsb + 1
  1329→        else:
  1330→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1331→                f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}",
  1332→                file=filepath
  1333→            )
  1334→
  1335→    elif isinstance(expr, Literal):
  1336→        return expr.value, expr.width
  1337→
  1338→    elif isinstance(expr, Concat):
  1339→        # Evaluate all parts
  1340→        parts_values = []
  1341→        total_width = 0
  1342→        for part in expr.parts:
  1343→            val, width = eval_expression(part, signal_values, signal_widths, filepath)
  1344→            parts_values.append((val, width))
  1345→            total_width += width
  1346→
  1347→        # Concatenate: first part is MSB
  1348→        result = 0
  1349→        for val, width in parts_values:
  1350→            result = (result << width) | val
  1351→
  1352→        return result, total_width
  1353→
  1354→    elif isinstance(expr, OpCall):
  1355→        op = expr.op.upper()
  1356→        args_results = []
  1357→        for arg in expr.args:
  1358→            val, width = eval_expression(arg, signal_values, signal_widths, filepath)
  1359→            args_results.append((val, width))
  1360→
  1361→        # Bitwise operators (width-preserving)
  1362→        if op in ('NOT', 'BUF'):
  1363→            if len(args_results) != 1:
  1364→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1365→                    f"Operator {op} requires 1 operand, got {len(args_results)}",
  1366→                    file=filepath
  1367→                )
  1368→            val, width = args_results[0]
  1369→            if op == 'NOT':
  1370→                return (~val) & ((1 << width) - 1), width
  1371→            else:  # BUF
  1372→                return val, width
  1373→
  1374→        elif op in ('AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'):
  1375→            if len(args_results) < 2:
  1376→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1377→                    f"Operator {op} requires at least 2 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
  1378→                    file=filepath
  1379→                )
  1380→
  1381→            # Check all operands have same width
  1382→            first_width = args_results[0][1]
  1383→            for val, width in args_results:
  1384→                if width != first_width:
  1385→                    raise WidthMismatchError(
  1386→                        f"Operator {op}: operand widths {width} and {first_width} don't match",
  1387→                        file=filepath
  1388→                    )
  1389→
  1390→            mask = (1 << first_width) - 1
  1391→            result = args_results[0][0]
  1392→
  1393→            if op == 'AND':
  1394→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1395→                    result &= val
  1396→            elif op == 'OR':
  1397→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1398→                    result |= val
  1399→            elif op == 'XOR':
  1400→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1401→                    result ^= val
  1402→            elif op == 'NAND':
  1403→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1404→                    result &= val
  1405→                result = mask & ~result
  1406→            elif op == 'NOR':
  1407→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1408→                    result |= val
  1409→                result = mask & ~result
  1410→            elif op == 'XNOR':
  1411→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1412→                    result ^= val
  1413→                result = mask & ~result
  1414→
  1415→            return result & mask, first_width
  1416→
  1417→        elif op in ('MUX', 'ITE'):
  1418→            if len(args_results) != 3:
  1419→                raise ArityError(
  1420→                    f"Operator {op} requires 3 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
  1421→                    file=filepath
  1422→                )
  1423→
  1424→            sel_val, sel_width = args_results[0]
  1425→            a_val, a_width = args_results[1]
  1426→            b_val, b_width = args_results[2]
  1427→
  1428→            if sel_width != 1:
  1429→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1430→                    f"Operator {op}: selector width {sel_width} must be 1",
  1431→                    file=filepath
  1432→                )
  1433→            if a_width != b_width:
  1434→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1435→                    f"Operator {op}: operand widths {a_width} and {b_width} don't match",
  1436→                    file=filepath
  1437→                )
  1438→
  1439→            result = a_val if sel_val else b_val
  1440→            return result, a_width
  1441→
  1442→        elif op in ('REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR'):
  1443→            if len(args_results) != 1:
  1444→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1445→                    f"Operator {op} requires 1 operand, got {len(args_results)}",
  1446→                    file=filepath
  1447→                )
  1448→
  1449→            val, width = args_results[0]
  1450→            if width < 1:
  1451→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1452→                    f"Operator {op}: operand width {width} must be >= 1",
  1453→                    file=filepath
  1454→                )
  1455→
  1456→            mask = (1 << width) - 1
  1457→            val &= mask
  1458→
  1459→            if op == 'REDUCE_AND':
  1460→                # Check if all bits are 1
  1461→                result = 1 if val == mask else 0
  1462→            elif op == 'REDUCE_OR':
  1463→                result = 1 if val != 0 else 0
  1464→            else:  # REDUCE_XOR
  1465→                result = 0
  1466→                for i in range(width):
  1467→                    result ^= (val >> i) & 1
  1468→
  1469→            return result, 1
  1470→
  1471→        elif op == 'EQ':
  1472→            if len(args_results) != 2:
  1473→                raise ArityError(
  1474→                    f"Operator EQ requires 2 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
  1475→                    file=filepath
  1476→                )
  1477→
  1478→            a_val, a_width = args_results[0]
  1479→            b_val, b_width = args_results[1]
  1480→
  1481→            if a_width != b_width:
  1482→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1483→                    f"Operator EQ: operand widths {a_width} and {b_width} don't match",
  1484→                    file=filepath
  1485→                )
  1486→
  1487→            return int(a_val == b_val), 1
  1488→
  1489→        else:
  1490→            raise ArityError(
  1491→                f"Unknown operator: {op}",
  1492→                file=filepath
  1493→            )
  1494→
  1495→    else:
  1496→        raise ValueError(f"Unknown AST node type: {type(expr)}")
  1497→
  1498→
  1499→def parse_input_literal(value_str: str, expected_width: int,
  1500→                        filepath: str, line: int = None, col: int = None) -> int:
  1501→    """Parse an input literal value and return the integer value.
  1502→
  1503→    Validates that the width matches the expected width.
  1504→    """
  1505→    if value_str in ('0', '1'):
  1506→        if expected_width != 1:
  1507→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1508→                f"Input width mismatch: got 1 but expected {expected_width}",
  1509→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1510→            )
  1511→        return int(value_str)
  1512→
  1513→    elif value_str.startswith('0b'):
  1514→        bits = value_str[2:]
  1515→        if not bits:
  1516→            raise InputValueParseError(
  1517→                f"Binary literal must have at least one digit",
  1518→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1519→            )
  1520→        value = int(bits, 2)
  1521→        width = len(bits)
  1522→
  1523→        if width != expected_width:
  1524→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1525→                f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
  1526→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1527→            )
  1528→        return value
  1529→
  1530→    elif value_str.startswith('0x'):
  1531→        hex_digits = value_str[2:]
  1532→        if not hex_digits:
  1533→            raise InputValueParseError(
  1534→                f"Hex literal must have at least one digit",
  1535→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1536→            )
  1537→        value = int(hex_digits, 16)
  1538→        width = len(hex_digits) * 4
  1539→
  1540→        if width != expected_width:
  1541→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1542→                f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
  1543→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1544→            )
  1545→        return value
  1546→
  1547→    else:
  1548→        # Sized literal: 8'b1010, 8'hff, 8'd200
  1549→        match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', value_str)
  1550→        if match:
  1551→            width = int(match.group(1))
  1552→            radix_str = match.group(2).lower()
  1553→            value_str_clean = match.group(3).replace('_', '')
  1554→
  1555→            if width != expected_width:
  1556→                raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1557→                    f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
  1558→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1559→                )
  1560→
  1561→            try:
  1562→                if radix_str == 'b':
  1563→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 2)
  1564→                elif radix_str == 'h':
  1565→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 16)
  1566→                else:  # 'd'
  1567→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 10)
  1568→            except ValueError:
  1569→                raise InputValueParseError(
  1570→                    f"Invalid literal: {value_str}",
  1571→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1572→                )
  1573→
  1574→            if value >= (1 << width):
  1575→                raise CircParseError(
  1576→                    f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal",
  1577→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1578→                )
  1579→
  1580→            return value
  1581→        else:
  1582→            raise InputValueParseError(
  1583→                f"Invalid input literal format: {value_str}",
  1584→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1585→            )
  1586→
  1587→
  1588→def format_vector_value(value: int, width: int, radix: str) -> str:
  1589→    """Format a vector value according to the specified radix.
  1590→
  1591→    Args:
  1592→        value: Integer value
  1593→        width: Bit width
  1594→        radix: 'bin', 'hex', or 'dec'
  1595→
  1596→    Returns:
  1597→        Formatted string
  1598→    """
  1599→    value &= (1 << width) - 1
  1600→
  1601→    if radix == 'bin':
  1602→        return f"0b{value:0{width}b}"
  1603→    elif radix == 'hex':
  1604→        # Calculate minimum number of hex digits
  1605→        hex_digits = (width + 3) // 4
  1606→        return f"0x{value:0{hex_digits}x}"
  1607→    elif radix == 'dec':
  1608→        return str(value)
  1609→

  1611→# =============================================================================
  1612→# Validation
  1613→# =============================================================================
  1614→
  1615→def validate_circ_file(filepath: str, declarations: List[SignalDecl],
  1616→                       assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]) -> Dict[str, object]:
  1617→    """Validate declarations and assignments, compute widths."""
  1618→
  1619→    # Track all declared signals
  1620→    inputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1621→    outputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1622→    wires: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1623→    all_signals: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1624→
  1625→    # Process declarations
  1626→    for decl in declarations:
  1627→        if decl.name in all_signals:
  1628→            raise DuplicateNameError(
  1629→                f"Duplicate signal name: {decl.name}",
  1630→                file=filepath
  1631→            )
  1632→        all_signals[decl.name] = decl
  1633→        if decl.sig_type == 'input':
  1634→            inputs[decl.name] = decl
  1635→        elif decl.sig_type == 'output':
  1636→            outputs[decl.name] = decl
  1637→        elif decl.sig_type == 'wire':
  1638→            wires[decl.name] = decl
  1639→
  1640→    # Track assignments
  1641→    assigned: Dict[str, Tuple[str, int]] = {}  # name -> (op, line_num)
  1642→
  1643→    # Build dependency graph for cycle detection
  1644→    graph: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
  1645→
  1646→    # Operator arities
  1647→    op_arity = {
  1648→        'NOT': 1,
  1649→        'BUF': 1,
  1650→        'AND': 2,
  1651→        'OR': 2,
  1652→        'XOR': 2,
  1653→        'NAND': 2,
  1654→        'NOR': 2,
  1655→        'XNOR': 2,
  1656→        'MUX': 3,
  1657→        'ITE': 3,
  1658→        'REDUCE_AND': 1,
  1659→        'REDUCE_OR': 1,
  1660→        'REDUCE_XOR': 1,
  1661→        'EQ': 2,
  1662→    }
  1663→
  1664→    for i, (lhs, expr) in enumerate(assignments):
  1665→        line_num = i + 1
  1666→
  1667→        # Check LHS is valid
  1668→        if lhs not in all_signals:
  1669→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1670→                f"Undefined signal: {lhs}",
  1671→                file=filepath
  1672→            )
  1673→
  1674→        signal_decl = all_signals[lhs]
  1675→        if signal_decl.sig_type == 'input':
  1676→            raise InputAssignmentError(
  1677→                f"Cannot assign to input: {lhs}",
  1678→                file=filepath
  1679→            )
  1680→
  1681→        # Check for multiple assignment
  1682→        if lhs in assigned:
  1683→            raise MultipleAssignmentError(
  1684→                f"Multiple assignment to: {lhs}",
  1685→                file=filepath
  1686→            )
  1687→
  1688→        assigned[lhs] = ('expr', line_num)
  1689→
  1690→        # Check expression and collect dependencies
  1691→        graph[lhs] = []
  1692→
  1693→        def collect_deps(node: ASTNode):
  1694→            if isinstance(node, SignalRef):
  1695→                if node.msb is not None:  # Has slice
  1696→                    # Check bounds against declared width if available
  1697→                    if node.name in all_signals:
  1698→                        decl = all_signals[node.name]
  1699→                        width = decl.width
  1700→                        if node.msb >= width or node.lsb >= width:
  1701→                            raise IndexOutOfBoundsError(
  1702→                                f"Index {node.msb}:{node.lsb} out of bounds for signal {node.name} (width {width})",
  1703→                                file=filepath
  1704→                            )
  1705→                if node.name not in ('0', '1'):
  1706→                    graph[lhs].append(node.name)
  1707→            elif isinstance(node, Literal):
  1708→                pass  # literals have no deps
  1709→            elif isinstance(node, Concat):
  1710→                for part in node.parts:
  1711→                    collect_deps(part)
  1712→            elif isinstance(node, OpCall):
  1713→                # Check arity
  1714→                op_upper = node.op.upper()
  1715→                if op_upper in op_arity:
  1716→                    min_arity = op_arity[op_upper]
  1717→                    if len(node.args) < min_arity:
  1718→                        raise ArityError(
  1719→                            f"Operator {node.op} requires at least {min_arity} operands, got {len(node.args)}",
  1720→                            file=filepath
  1721→                        )
  1722→                else:
  1723→                    raise ArityError(
  1724→                        f"Unknown operator: {node.op}",
  1725→                        file=filepath
  1726→                    )
  1727→                for arg in node.args:
  1728→                    collect_deps(arg)
  1729→
  1730→        try:
  1731→            collect_deps(expr)
  1732→        except (IndexOutOfBoundsError, ArityError):
  1733→            raise
  1734→        except Exception as e:
  1735→            raise CircParseError(
  1736��                f"Error in expression: {e}",
  1737→                file=filepath
  1738→            )
  1739→
  1740→    # Check for unassigned signals (only wires and outputs)
  1741→    for name, sig_type in [('wire', wires), ('output', outputs)]:
  1742→        for decl in all_signals.values():
  1743→            if decl.sig_type in ('wire', 'output'):
  1744→                if decl.name not in assigned:
  1745→                    raise UnassignedSignalError(
  1746→                        f"Unassigned signal: {decl.name}",
  1747→                        file=filepath
  1748→                    )
  1749→
  1750→    # Check for cycles
  1751→    def find_cycle() -> Optional[List[str]]:
  1752→        visited: Set[str] = set()
  1753→        rec_stack: List[str] = []
  1754→
  1755→        def dfs(node: str) -> Optional[List[str]]:
  1756→            visited.add(node)
  1757→            rec_stack.append(node)
  1758→
  1759→            for neighbor in graph.get(node, []):
  1760→                if neighbor not in visited:
  1761→                    result = dfs(neighbor)
  1762→                    if result:
  1763→                        return result
  1764→                elif neighbor in rec_stack:
  1765→                    start_idx = rec_stack.index(neighbor)
  1766→                    return rec_stack[start_idx:] + [node]
  1767→
  1768→            rec_stack.pop()
  1769→            return None
  1770→
  1771→        for node in graph:
  1772→            if node not in visited:
  1773→                cycle = dfs(node)
  1774→                if cycle:
  1775→                    return cycle
  1776→        return None
  1777→
  1778→    cycle = find_cycle()
  1779→    if cycle:
  1780→        cycle_path = " -> ".join(cycle)
  1781→        raise CycleError(
  1782→            f"Cycle detected: {cycle_path}",
  1783→            file=filepath
  1784→        )
  1785→
  1786→    # Return success info
  1787→    sorted_inputs = sorted(inputs.values(), key=lambda d: d.name)
  1788→    sorted_outputs = sorted(outputs.values(), key=lambda d: d.name)
  1789→
  1790→    return {
  1791→        'inputs': [{'name': d.name, 'msb': d.msb, 'lsb': d.lsb} for d in sorted_inputs],
  1792→        'outputs': [{'name': d.name, 'msb': d.msb, 'lsb': d.lsb} for d in sorted_outputs],
  1793→    }
  1794→
  1795→

  1800→def format_error_json(error: CircuitError, command: str) -> str:
  1801→    """Format error as JSON envelope."""
  1802→    error_type = type(error).__name__
  1803→    result = {
  1804→        'ok': False,
  1805→        'command': command,
  1806→        'exit_code': error.exit_code,
  1807→        'error': {
  1808→            'type': error_type,
  1809→            'message': error.message,
  1810→            'file': error.file,
  1811→            'line': error.line,
  1812→            'col': error.col,
  1813→        }
  1814→    }
  1815→    return json.dumps(result)
  1816→
  1817→
  1818→def format_version_json() -> str:
  1819→    """Format version output as JSON."""
  1820→    return json.dumps({
  1821→        'ok': True,
  1822→        'command': '__version__',
  1823→        'version': VERSION,
  1824→    })
  1825→
  1826→
  1827→def detect_format(filepath: str, format_override: str) -> str:
  1828→    """Detect circuit format from file extension or override.
  1829→
  1830→    Args:
  1831→        filepath: Path to the circuit file
  1832→        format_override: One of 'auto', 'circ', 'json', 'bench'
  1833→
  1834→    Returns:
  1835→        Format string: 'circ', 'json', or 'bench'
